title: sempervivum hirtum subsp. neilreichii
intro: Sempervivum hirtum subsp. neilreichii, also known as Neilreich's houseleek, is a subspecies of the Sempervivum hirtum plant, which belongs to the succulent family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Neilreich's houseleek include reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and supporting the immune system, making it a popular choice for treating various health conditions. The plant is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, salves, and ointments, to take advantage of its medicinal properties, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
